findings.title: XSS via innerHTML in dashboard
findings.description: Six innerHTML assignments render unsanitized server-supplied strings directly into the DOM. If any task/thread/event data contains script tags or event-handler attributes, they will execute in the user's browser. The dashboard is served from the harness HTTP server and displays live agent output.
findings.action: Replace innerHTML with textContent for plain-text fields. For fields that genuinely require HTML structure, build DOM nodes with createElement/appendChild or use a sanitizer like DOMPurify. Audit which fields are user-controlled versus server-generated.

findings.title: Path traversal in sandbox boundary enforcement
findings.description: Eight path-handling sites in the sandbox crate use user-supplied paths without canonicalizing them or verifying they remain within the allowed base directory. The sandbox crate is the security boundary that prevents agents from escaping their project root; a path traversal here defeats the entire containment model (e.g. '../../../etc/passwd').
findings.action: Apply std::fs::canonicalize() then assert the result starts_with(allowed_base). Return a permission-denied error if it does not. Add tests with '../' sequences confirming they are rejected.

findings.title: Path traversal in event log file paths
findings.description: Four path constructions in event_store.rs accept caller-supplied path components without canonicalization. An attacker who can inject the project_root or session_id could write event log files to arbitrary locations outside the data directory.
findings.action: Canonicalize all constructed paths and assert they are children of the base data directory before opening or writing.

findings.title: Nested lock acquisition deadlock risk in project service
findings.description: Four sites in services/project.rs acquire one lock while holding another. Under concurrent task execution, if any caller acquires the same locks in a different order, the server will deadlock. This is especially hazardous in the project service which is called from both HTTP handlers and background tasks.
findings.action: Audit all lock sites and establish a strict global lock order. Restructure callers to acquire locks in the canonical order, or use tokio::sync::RwLock with try_read/try_write and back-off to avoid inversion.

findings.title: Nested lock acquisition deadlock in task runner
findings.description: task_runner.rs acquires a second lock while already holding one during task lifecycle transitions. The task runner is a critical hot path — a deadlock here stalls all agent execution.
findings.action: Drop the outer lock guard before acquiring the inner one. Alternatively refactor to use a single coarser-grained lock or a lock-free structure for the state that requires nesting.

findings.title: Architecture violation: direct git CLI calls in harness server crate
findings.description: periodic_reviewer.rs contains five tokio::process::Command::new("git") calls (log, ls-files, diff). CLAUDE.md mandates: 'ZERO Command::new("gh") or Command::new("git") calls inside harness crates — all GitHub/git interaction must be in agent prompts only.' This is a freshly landed violation in the commit 'feat: structured review agent with JSON output, finding persistence, and decompose bypass'. Direct git invocation makes the server depend on a git binary in PATH and breaks the prompt-only delegation model.
findings.action: Move git context gathering (repo structure, diff-stat, recent commits) into the agent prompt itself: instruct the review agent to run 'git ls-files', 'git diff --stat', 'git log --oneline' as part of its review workflow. Remove gather_repo_structure, gather_diff_stat, gather_recent_commits from periodic_reviewer.rs.

findings.title: TOCTOU race in exec handler map insertion
findings.description: handlers/exec.rs uses get() + insert() on a DashMap, creating a check-then-act race. Under concurrent requests for the same key, two goroutines can both see 'not present', both proceed to compute the value, and the second write silently overwrites the first. This can cause duplicate execution or lost state.
findings.action: Use DashMap's entry().or_insert() / or_insert_with() API to make the check-and-insert atomic.

findings.title: Dual Task*/Todo* domain model coexistence
findings.description: Task* and Todo* types coexist across task_runner.rs, task_db.rs, task_queue.rs, intake/mod.rs, and core/agent.rs. This creates confusion about which model represents the source of truth, and risks data divergence when updates are applied to only one model. The guard scan flagged 15 sites in main-branch files.
findings.action: Consolidate to a single domain model. Audit all Todo* usages; if they map 1-to-1 to Task*, delete Todo* and update callsites. If they serve a distinct purpose, document the semantic difference explicitly with a comment.

findings.title: 24 unwrap() calls in webhook handler — high panic risk
findings.description: webhook.rs has the highest unwrap() density in the codebase (24 sites). Webhook handlers receive external HTTP traffic; any malformed payload or unexpected state will panic the server thread. This is the most critical unwrap cluster because the data source is fully external and untrusted.
findings.action: Replace each unwrap() with ? propagation or explicit match/if-let. Return HTTP 400 for client-caused errors and HTTP 500 (with logging) for internal failures.

findings.title: 33 unwrap() calls in lang_detect — regex/parse panics
findings.description: lang_detect.rs has the highest absolute count of unwrap() calls (33). Most are likely on Regex::new() or similar infallible-looking operations. If any regex fails to compile or a parse assumption is violated, the whole server crashes. This module is invoked on every incoming task.
findings.action: Initialize regexes with lazy_static or once_cell::sync::Lazy so compilation failures surface at startup rather than at call time. Replace runtime unwrap() on parse operations with proper error handling.

findings.title: 20 unwrap() calls in recently-added post_validator
findings.description: post_validator.rs was added recently and already contains 20 unwrap() calls. Post-validation is a correctness gate — panics here would silently skip validation rather than surfacing errors.
findings.action: Audit all unwrap() in post_validator.rs. Use ? to propagate errors to the caller so validation failures are reported, not panicked.

findings.title: 8 silent Result discards in WebSocket layer
findings.description: websocket.rs silently discards errors via .ok() or .unwrap_or_default() at 8 sites. WebSocket send errors (client disconnect, back-pressure) are dropped with no trace. This makes it impossible to diagnose connection issues in production.
findings.action: Replace .ok() with if let Err(e) = ... { tracing::warn!(...) } at each site. For sends in async loops, use a break or return on error to avoid writing to a closed connection.

findings.title: 11 hardcoded DB path constructions in task_db.rs
findings.description: task_db.rs constructs SQLite paths inline in 11 places rather than calling a shared default_db_path() function from harness-core. This matches the pattern across thread_db.rs, plan_db.rs, project_registry.rs, router.rs and others (80+ total violations). Multiple DB files can end up in different locations depending on which code path runs first, causing data fragmentation.
findings.action: Implement default_db_path() in harness-core::config::dirs (already flagged by U-11 guard). Update all DB-opening callers to use it. Add an integration test that starts two different entry points and verifies they open the same physical file.

findings.title: Deduplication only updates review_id, loses finding metadata drift
findings.description: In persist_findings(), when a recurring finding is detected (same rule_id + file), only the review_id is updated. The title, description, action, impact, and priority fields are NOT updated. If a finding's severity or remediation advice changes between review runs, the stored record stays stale.
findings.action: In the UPDATE branch, also update priority, impact, confidence, effort, title, description, and action to the latest values so recurring findings reflect the most current assessment.

findings.title: Orphaned polling spawns accumulate if review tasks overlap
findings.description: run_review_tick() spawns an async polling loop (line 157) to wait for task completion, then returns Ok(()). If the next periodic tick fires before the previous task completes, a second polling loop is spawned concurrently. Over time, many overlapping polling loops accumulate in the runtime, all watching different task_ids. The max_wait guard limits duration per-loop but not total concurrency.
findings.action: Use a Mutex<Option<JoinHandle>> to track the active polling task; cancel or skip spawning a new one if one is already running. Alternatively, restructure so the periodic tick skips if the previous review task is still in-flight.
